page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 12.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps under a KVM hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.ic5.3xlarge demonstrates average multi-core processing capabilities, as evidenced by its middle-tier performance in multi-threaded Geekbench, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression tests. However, single-core performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average range, though Passmark memory scores are weak.

The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is defined by its high compute density relative to its memory allocation. By offering 12 dedicated vCPUs with only 12.0 GB of RAM, it provides a resource profile optimized for workloads that require parallel CPU processing without the overhead of unused memory. This makes it a cost-effective option for parallelizable tasks that are not memory-intensive.

Appropriate workloads for the ecs.ic5.3xlarge include multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and parallelized batch processing. It is also suitable for Redis operations and basic database tasks that fit within the 12.0 GB memory limit, but it is poorly suited for memory-heavy databases or large-scale language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.3xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 12.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU hardware, it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, software compilation, and static web serving capabilities, contrasted by weak single-core CPU and memory performance. With a strict 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads that do not demand large memory footprints, such as multi-threaded batch processing, lightweight database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.3xlarge is a compute-focused instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 12.0 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for parallelized, low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.3xlarge features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 12.0 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded, low-memory compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core compute instance designed for parallelized workloads requiring minimal memory overhead and steady multi-threaded performance.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs with a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
12.0 GB of system memory and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance in software compilation and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing and lightweight database operations
